name: Skillet Chocolate Fudge
recipeIngredient: 5 1/2 oz Unsweetened baking chocolate - cut into chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, heavy-bottomed frying pan, mix sugar and half-and-half. Bring to boil over low heat.
2. Add corn syrup and stir gently a few times. Insert candy thermometer and cook without stirring until it registers between 236 and 238 degrees (on humid days or if your kitchen is humid, cook to the higher temperature). Do not remove thermometer.
3. Remove frying pan from heat and allow to cool. Sprinkle chocolate pieces over hot syrup and add a chunk of butter. Do not stir.
4. Allow chocolate and butter to melt. When syrup has cooled to 110 degrees on candy thermometer, remove thermometer.
5. Spoon and scrape contents of pan into bowl of electric mixer. Add vanilla and salt; turn mixer on low speed. Beat until candy begins to harden.
6. Immediately scrape and spoon candy out of mixing bowl onto flat surface; it will still be warm. With both hands, knead candy for a moment, then knead in pecans.
7. Line an 8x8-inch square pan with plastic wrap. Press fudge firmly into pan, flattening it on top and pressing it into corners.
8. Cover tightly and let sit for about 6 to 8 hours or overnight. Remove fudge from pan using plastic wrap. With a sharp knife, cut fudge into 8 lengthwise rows, then make 6 more rows at right angles to the first cuts.
Note: If peppermint fudge is desired, omit vanilla and substitute 1/4 teaspoon peppermint extract or to taste.
